blocks.content: Uorin scratched the floor where Shirone had fallen with her fingers.
A transparent sheet of film peeled off with a 'zip' sound, and she held it in her hand.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Ta-da! This is it. Now, check the floor again.
When Shirone stepped on it, unlike when he fell, the hardness of the marble that matched common sense was transmitted.
He even stomped down with his full weight, but the result was the same.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Isn't that amazing? With just this, you can take a nap even on rocky terrain. You can even do this. Watch closely.
Uorin walked up to the eagle sculpture that was taller than her and covered it with the film.
As if by magic, the sculpture sank and a flat floor appeared.
She pressed down her skirt, jumped up, and landed on her bottom.
Her buttocks sank into the fluffy floor and bounced back.
Her actions were childish, but Shirone observed her seriously.
He had no idea what caused this to happen.
It wasn't science. But it wasn't magic either.
Shirone looked around seriously.
Only then did he realize why the items in the room were not luxurious.
This was a place where the most expensive items in the world were gathered.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: That's right. This is where we collect Objet.
He had suspected it, but hearing the words spoken out loud sent a wave of shock through him.
He recalled the Objet of Sleep owned by the two-horned Mara Baalb at Heaven.
Even then, he shuddered at its strangeness, but the current shock was on a different level.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Are you saying everything here is an Objet?
In any normal place, items would be arranged according to their function—tableware in the kitchen, books in the library.
But here, there was no order or reason for how things were displayed, and there was no consistency in their arrangement.
Roughly counting, there were over 50 items on display.
It was only possible because they were the Terraje Family that ruled the continent.
While Shirone was still in a daze, Zion approached, holding a copy of Spirit magazine.
As expected, his reaction was cynical.
Raising the corners of his mouth as if it was nothing, he shoved the magazine and said,

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: A genius aspiring magician, huh?
Shirone didn't even feel like responding.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Of course, magic is a tremendous power. I acknowledge that too.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: But you see, even such an ability is nothing compared to an Objet, right?
The annual salary of a 3rd-class archmage working at Kazra Royal Palace is approximately 30 million gold.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: If I sell one of these items, how many archmages could I hire?
What do you want to say?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: No matter how outstanding a mage is, in the end, they're just a dog of the royalty.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: The talent of a ruler isn't determined by something trivial like magic.
Zion held the Spirit magazine at Shirone's eye level and dropped it.
He stepped on the magazine and walked past Shirone, turning around.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Must feel great, huh? In three days, you'll be the first prince.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What's it like? Already feeling like a king?
Now that the paternity test was over, it was clear that the power struggle among the nobles would intensify.
In such a situation, it was natural for someone like Zion, who was at the heart of power, to gauge Shirone's state of mind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm pretty sure I made it clear. I'm not interested.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As soon as the results are out, I'm going back home.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Hmph, sure you will.
Zion didn't believe him.
Who in the world would turn down the throne?
Even if someone did, it would only be because they hadn't yet tasted the sweetness of power.
Power guarantees all kinds of pleasures that humans crave.
Once he becomes the first prince, no matter how much character Shirone has cultivated, his mind was bound to change.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: It's no use pretending to be noble.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: This is the royal palace.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Especially with me, such tricks won't work.
Zion snatched Fluffy Anywhere from Uorin and threw it on the floor.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: For one toy like this, I could buy the entire magic academy you attend to.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: In an instant, I can become your teacher.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Your teacher will have to crawl between my legs.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: And what?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: You're not interested in the throne? Do you think I'd believe that?
Shirone realized that Zion was a person purely driven by material things.
Shirone attended the academy not for tangible things but to gain the intangible values that lay within.
Hearing the story, it seemed that Fluffy Everywhere was in the billions range,
but he just dismissed it as a child's toy.
But the power of an Objet, which defied common sense, could hold immense value depending on how it was used.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: For example, what if it was used to conceal a vault containing the Essence of Spirits?
No matter how expensive an Objet is, it's not even worth a dime compared to the Essence of Spirits, of which there are only 17 in the world.

Once equipped, not only could one cast magic of the corresponding element, but they could also completely block magic of that same element, making the Essences worth more than the entire budget of a small kingdom.
A family like Terraje might be able to buy it for amusement,
but Zion's belief that they were expensive purely because of a collector's whim was simply a misconception.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I think it would be better if you gave the Fluffy Anywhere to someone else.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter how good a book is, it's useless to someone who would use it as a pot coaster.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Haha! That's the privilege of royalty.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: A good book? We could use gold as a pot coaster if we felt like it.
Shirone gritted his teeth and swallowed his anger.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Enough. If you're done talking, I'll be going back.
Staying any longer would only hurt his feelings, so he headed towards the door,
but Uorin ran over and tugged on his sleeve.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Wait! I have something to tell Shirone Oppa too.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: There's something I want to show you. Come this way.
Shirone found it hard to refuse Uorin.
First of all, she was someone he could communicate with,
and she had supported him among the arrogant royalty.
Above all, she was the daughter of the mighty Terraje Family.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Hurry, hurry. This way.
As Shirone was dragged away by Uorin,
Zion sneered,
but in reality, his situation wasn't all that different from Shirone's.
Seeing Zion silently follow his sister after she mentioned she had something to do was another sign of his circumstances.
Uorin sat Shirone at the table and brought a small box.
It had a shabby square wooden frame with a concave hemispherical interior structure.
In the center was a worn-out arrow-shaped pointer, which seemed to be a roulette that could be flicked and spun with a finger.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: This is an Objet called Daily Gift.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: It leaves a gift once a day.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huhu, strange, isn't it?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: At first, everyone feels that way.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Since Objets operate beyond the bounds of common sense, when encountering the 'Guideline,' things often don't make sense.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's right. The strangest part is when you say it leaves. Who exactly?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is just an object.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Guideline contains two meanings.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: First, for something to be 'given,' gravity is necessary.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Secondly, 'giving' implies leaving but not arriving.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Therefore, there needs to be a space that can't be observed from inside or outside.
Shirone realized why the entrance here had a double structure.
There had to be a third location where no one was passing through, neither from the inside nor the outside.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: <Gravity> and <No Observer>. If these two conditions are met, it gives a gift once a day.
Shirone's gaze followed where Uorin pointed.
It was an old item, with the paint fading,
but the different shades were still distinguishable.
The size of the areas varied, and there was writing engraved inside.
It was faint and hard to see, but it was definitely not in the Thormian language.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: The letters written here are in the language used by the gypsies of the eastern mountain region.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Translated, it's Modest Fift, Pleasant Gift, Excessive Gift, and Miracle.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: When measured in radians, Modest Gift takes up about 75 percent,

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Pleasant Gift is 20 percent,

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Excessive Gift is 4 percent,

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: and Miracle is 1 percent of the space.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm….
The fact that Modest Gift took up 75 percent suggested that the gap between the different levels of gifts was quite significant.
The 4 percent chance for Excessive Gift was exciting,
but Shirone was even more curious about what a Miracle would be.
Since the roulette could only be spun once a day,
simple math suggested it would land on a Miracle within 100 days.
However, when considering the concept of probability,
it could take much longer than that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Could I spin this arrow?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Yes. That's why I was waiting for Oppa to come.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Give it a spin. I'll give Oppa the gift that comes out.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Given Uorin's wealth,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: no matter how great the gift, she likely wouldn't be too attached to it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: However, since no one knew what would come out,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: it didn't seem like something one would easily give away.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Probability, huh…
In any case, it was an opportunity to directly experience an Objet.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone didn't particularly desire any gift for himself,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: but he was intensely curious to see what would come out.
Perhaps, that curiosity was the true value of this Objet?
The thrill that comes with imagining the gift.
In that sense, Daily Gift was a very well-designed entertainment tool that understood human psychology.
Unlike other forms of gambling, there was no failure.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: At the very least, a Modest Gift is guaranteed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, can I give it a try?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Of course. I do it every day anyway.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: You just need to flick the end of the pointer with your finger.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Based on experiments, it needs to spin at least ten full rotations;

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: otherwise, it's considered invalid.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But don't worry, it doesn't mean you lose your chance for the day if it doesn't spin enough.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh-ho, I see.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why must it spin at least ten times?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If there was no limit on the number of rotations,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: you could get a Miracle just by gently pushing the gauge.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's really amazing. Even though it's an object,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: it has rules like Rule Eater.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This, maybe it can be made to always land on a Miracle.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Exactly. Or, you could hire a professional gambler.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: By feeling the sensitivity of the roulette and adjusting the force,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: you could always land on the same result.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: That's a great idea.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: But unfortunately, it's impossible.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: I've already tried.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: It seems that when you spin the roulette,

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: the position is determined by some kind of random number generation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, so you're saying that Daily Gift is manipulating the probabilities?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even the 1% chance of a Miracle might be a trick.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it's a gambling Objet,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: at least the probability should be fair.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Otherwise, there's no need to have an interface to experiment with probability in the first place.
Uorin felt a strong sense of kinship with Shirone.
In fact, she had made several people besides Shirone spin the roulette.
But most of them were more focused on the gift they would receive rather than being fascinated by the nature of the Objet.
There were times when she got annoyed and left after seeing them praying for expensive gifts in front of the roulette.
They say that an Objet collector needs two things: an interesting Object and an audience who can appreciate its true value.
Zion occasionally found decent Objects for her,
but when it came to discernment, he was completely clueless.
